title: 奉命谋杀
summary:
Philip and Mary Darland continue their discussion regarding the suspects in the murder investigation. Mary eliminates the victim's father and Hester from her list of suspects, narrowing the possibilities down to Cora and Gwendolyn. Philip analyzes Cora, describing her as a plain woman driven by a desire for marriage and social status, suggesting she may have overstayed her role as a massage therapist. Philip also suspects Tina, noting her quiet, elegant demeanor and a strange comment she made about wishing she knew nothing about the situation. Additionally, Philip expresses an interest in investigating Korsleigh, whom he perceives as simple-minded and honest. Despite Mary's visible distress and her pleas for him to abandon his investigation and return home, Philip decides to stay until the weekend to continue his analysis.

In a separate scene, Mickey informs his father, Leo Aigle, that both he and Tina will be staying for several more days. Mickey expresses sudden gratitude toward Leo, reflecting on his past ungratefulness and his decision to pursue a career in vehicle maintenance rather than the oil industry favored by his mother. Leo discusses a discretionary trust fund established years ago for Mickey, explaining that the money belongs to him as an adopted son. This revelation causes Mickey visible distress; he insists he does not want to touch the money, though he cannot clearly explain his reasoning. The conversation is interrupted when Philip arrives, maneuvering his wheelchair into the room.
